domain: Mongolian/Kazakh dombra (two-string lute; melody string + drone string tuned to a fixed interval; simultaneous dynamic+stable channels with drone providing stable reference)
specific_mechanism: Dual-channel simultaneous output — one dynamic melody string + one stable drone reference at fixed interval; drone provides comparison anchor against which melody is heard.
llm_application: DOMBRA-CONSISTENCY-EVAL: a paired-prompt consistency diagnostic. For each evaluation case, issue (P) a TEST PROMPT and (Q) a CANONICAL REFERENCE PROMPT (semantically equivalent, canonical phrasing). Run both through model; compute f_P, f_Q. Diagnostic score combines (a) consistency-drift = D(f_P, f_Q) and (b) correctness = D(f_P, ground_truth). Both required low for good score. Reveals prompt-brittleness orthogonal to accuracy. Differs from single-prompt eval (no reference) and prompt-brittleness benchmarks (which measure variance across many paraphrases, not against a canonical) by SINGLE-PAIRED comparison against ANCHOR PROMPT.
novelty_claim: Prompt-brittleness studies measure variance across N paraphrases. PromptBench evaluates robustness. PromptRobust + AdvPrompt do adversarial paraphrase. LLM Readiness Harness aggregates multi-dimensional scores. DOMBRA-CONSISTENCY-EVAL differs by (a) PAIRED single test + canonical reference, (b) JOINT correctness + drift score, (c) FIXED-INTERVAL expected response distance, (d) compact diagnostic compared with broader brittleness benchmarks.
